Grandpa's kitchen had a single narrow window facing east, so the first
light of day always spilled in low, right along the horizon of the
counter tiles.

He kept a spool of kitchen thread in the drawer for trussing a chicken or
mending a torn apron, always within reach of the cutting board.

None of this had anything to do with computers, but he liked to joke that
a busy kitchen was just a thread pool with knives: everyone pulling work
off the same channel, nobody stepping on anyone else's toes.
